text.title: Spec → Plan → Tasks: the only legal order
text.summary: The agent must produce spec.md before plan.md, plan.md before tasks.md, and may write code only when driven by tasks.md.

section.title: Fixed artifact order
section.p: Spec-kit defines a sequenced pipeline. Each artifact has a different audience, a different gate, and a different update rule. Skipping a stage collapses the contract: the agent loses the WHAT/WHY, the human loses the HOW review, and the merged code has no traceability anchor.

rule.statement: The agent MUST run `/speckit.specify` before `/speckit.plan`, and `/speckit.plan` before `/speckit.tasks`. The agent MUST NOT edit any source file until `tasks.md` exists, lists the file in scope, and is checked into the feature branch. Any unresolved `[NEEDS CLARIFICATION]` marker in `spec.md` blocks plan generation.
rule.rationale: The spec is the only artifact that survives model swaps and conversation truncation; the plan is the only artifact that records architectural rationale for review; tasks.md is the only artifact that decomposes the work into parallelizable, AC-anchored units the coding agent can attempt independently. Reordering or skipping breaks the audit chain.

example.code: # feature branch: feat/oauth-google $ /speckit.specify "Add OAuth Google login for end-users" # → spec.md generated with 4 [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] markers # Resolve markers via PM input → re-run specify → 0 markers $ /speckit.plan # → plan.md picks Authlib, sets redirect rules, records constitution gates $ /speckit.tasks # → tasks.md emits 11 work items, T-003 and T-007 marked [P] # Only NOW does the coding agent open editor on auth/views.py.
example.note: Each command produces a versioned artifact; coding starts only after tasks.md.

example.code: # Skip the chain — go straight to code $ claude "implement Google OAuth login" # Agent guesses Authlib vs python-social-auth, picks redirect URI rule # without product review, ships code. Two weeks later PM says # "we wanted PKCE-only, no client secret in env." # Rework cost: 100% of the original work.
example.note: No spec, no plan, no tasks → no rollback anchor and no traceable AC.

antipattern.description: Agent autopopulates `[NEEDS CLARIFICATION]` markers with plausible defaults to "make progress" instead of blocking on PM resolution.
antipattern.reason: Defaults stuffed into the spec become invisible assumptions in the merged code; the constitution's "test-first" gate cannot fail what it cannot see.

section.reference: GitHub blog: Spec-driven development with AI (Sept 2025).
